title: Mona Lisa
summary: Mystery, ambiguity, the inscrutable smile — about reading what isn't said.

title: The Creation of Adam
summary: The fingertip-spark of life — origins, beginnings, the gap before something exists.

title: The School of Athens
summary: A gathering of philosophers — ideas, debate, intellectual tradition.

title: The Birth of Venus
summary: Beauty arriving fully-formed — emergence, the new entering the world.

title: Primavera
summary: Spring, renewal, the season of beginnings.

title: The Garden of Earthly Delights
summary: Excess, temptation, the chaotic abundance of desire — what too much choice looks like.

title: Vitruvian Man
summary: First principles — measuring the world against what is true.

title: The Arnolfini Portrait
summary: A binding contract, witnessed; ceremony, commitment, the weight of partnership.

title: Melencolia I
summary: The thinker stalled — frustration, blocked genius, work that won't move.

title: The Tower of Babel
summary: Overreach, ambition collapsing under its own weight, communication breaking down.

title: Hunters in the Snow
summary: Returning home empty-handed, persistence in the cold — the labor that doesn't pay off today.

title: The Night Watch
summary: A team rallying, mid-motion, the moment before action.

title: The Anatomy Lesson of Dr. Nicolaes Tulp
summary: Dissecting to understand — first-principles inquiry, deep examination.

title: Belshazzar's Feast
summary: The writing on the wall — the moment you read a warning too late.

title: The Storm on the Sea of Galilee
summary: Crisis on the boat, the founder steering through the storm.

title: Girl with a Pearl Earring
summary: Quiet attention, the look of someone really listening.

title: The Milkmaid
summary: Daily craft, focused labor, the dignity of small things done well.

title: Las Meninas
summary: Layers of looking — frames within frames, the observer observed.

title: Judith Slaying Holofernes
summary: Decisive action — the moment of irreversible commitment.

title: Liberty Leading the People
summary: Movement, banner-bearing, leading from the front — founder mode.

title: The Raft of the Medusa
summary: Survival in the wake of disaster — the struggle no one warned you about.

title: Wanderer above the Sea of Fog
summary: Standing alone above the unknown — vision, contemplation, the founder gazing out.

title: The Third of May 1808
summary: Confrontation with the inevitable — the choice that defines you.

title: Saturn Devouring His Son
summary: The thing you built consuming you — paranoia, internal threat.

title: The Sleep of Reason Produces Monsters
summary: What happens when the mind stops thinking — illusions multiplying in the dark.

title: The Fighting Temeraire
summary: The old giant being towed to be broken up — disruption, generational shift.

title: Rain, Steam and Speed
summary: Velocity, the future blurring toward you — technology in motion.

title: The Death of Socrates
summary: Choosing principle over self-preservation — conviction tested.

title: Napoleon Crossing the Alps
summary: Mythic ambition — the founder as conqueror, the legend the brand wants.

title: The Gleaners
summary: The quiet dignity of unscalable manual work.

title: The Stone Breakers
summary: Hard labor, the gritty foundation no one sees.

title: Barge Haulers on the Volga
summary: Hauling a heavy thing forward together — the slog of building.

title: Ivan the Terrible and His Son Ivan
summary: Catastrophic regret — the consequence of an unrecoverable choice.

title: Reply of the Zaporozhian Cossacks
summary: A team howling with laughter as they write their reply — the joy of telling power to get lost.

title: Impression, Sunrise
summary: The dawn of a new movement — the painting that named it.

title: Water Lilies
summary: Returning again and again to the same subject — patient mastery.

title: Luncheon of the Boating Party
summary: An early team enjoying the work — the founding lunch table.

title: Bal du moulin de la Galette
summary: A community in motion — the kind of place ideas spread by talking.

title: A Sunday on La Grande Jatte
summary: Painting built dot by dot — the discipline of doing things that don't scale.

title: The Starry Night
summary: Vision burning at night — the founder unable to stop thinking.

title: Sunflowers
summary: Doing the same thing twelve times to find what works.

title: Café Terrace at Night
summary: The quiet hour after work — reflection, third places, the conversation after the meeting.

title: Wheatfield with Crows
summary: An ending in motion — endings, last work, finality.

title: The Card Players
summary: Hand against hand, calculation, the stillness of a high-stakes decision.

title: Mont Sainte-Victoire
summary: Returning to the same mountain again — fixation, the search for the right form.

title: The Sleeping Gypsy
summary: Rest in the wilderness, the lion that didn't strike — the danger that didn't materialize.

title: The Scream
summary: Anxiety, the moment the future arrives — the panic of the founder.

title: The Kiss
summary: Devotion, golden focus, the singular passion.

title: Portrait of Adele Bloch-Bauer I
summary: Wealth made visible — the painting that became the price tag.

title: The Old Guitarist
summary: Mastery in poverty — the years of unrewarded craft.

title: Composition VII
summary: Pure abstraction, complexity finding its own order.

title: Nighthawks
summary: Late-night work, separation by glass — the loneliness of building.

title: Automat
summary: A woman alone with her thoughts — interior life, the pause inside the day.

title: Office at Night
summary: Working late, the tension of doing the work no one sees.

title: Christina's World
summary: The long pull toward something distant — endurance, agency under constraint.

title: American Gothic
summary: A stoic founding couple — the partnership behind the operation.

title: The Third-Class Carriage
summary: Strangers on a long journey — the patience of getting somewhere together.

title: The Great Wave off Kanagawa
summary: The crest before the break — the moment before disruption hits.

title: South Wind, Clear Sky (Red Fuji)
summary: The mountain at dawn, calm and inevitable — long-term thinking made visible.

title: The Thinker (Rodin sculpture)
summary: Thought as posture — the founder mid-decision.

title: Landscape with the Fall of Icarus
summary: Disaster ignored by everyone going about their day — the failure no one noticed.

title: Bedroom in Arles
summary: A small space, a clear head — the room where the work gets done.

title: The Potato Eaters
summary: The early-stage team eating what they grew — bootstrapping.

title: View of Toledo
summary: A city under a coming storm — the early signs of a shift.

title: The Ambassadors
summary: Status symbols and a hidden skull — the cost behind the prestige.

title: The Calling of Saint Matthew
summary: The finger of fate pointing at someone mid-routine — the unexpected tap on the shoulder.

title: The Supper at Emmaus
summary: The moment of recognition at the table — when you finally see what's been in front of you.

title: Boy with a Basket of Fruit
summary: A young craftsman showing his early work — the apprentice years.

title: The Return of the Prodigal Son
summary: The welcome after the long detour — coming home after failing publicly.

title: Aristotle with a Bust of Homer
summary: A hand on a predecessor's head — standing on the shoulders of giants.

title: View of Delft
summary: Your hometown seen in perfect light — the place that shaped everything.

title: The Astronomer
summary: Reaching to touch a model of the heavens — building a private theory of how the world works.

title: The Geographer
summary: Mid-thought, mapping the unknown — the founder plotting the next move.

title: Ophelia
summary: Drifting downstream, surrounded by detail — beautiful collapse.

title: The Lady of Shalott
summary: Leaving the tower for the world, knowing the cost — the choice to be seen.

title: Portrait of Madame X
summary: A pose calibrated for attention — the painting that nearly ended a career.

title: Carnation, Lily, Lily, Rose
summary: Painted only at dusk, a few minutes a day — the discipline of waiting for the right light.

title: The Daughters of Edward Darley Boit
summary: Four girls placed across a room of empty space — the unspoken hierarchy of a family business.

title: Whistler's Mother
summary: A study in patience and restraint — the long quiet of waiting for the work to land.

title: A Bar at the Folies-Bergère
summary: The barmaid facing forward, reflected in a mirror that doesn't quite match — the public face vs. the private one.

title: Olympia
summary: A direct stare that broke convention — the work that scandalized the room.

title: The Luncheon on the Grass
summary: Rejected by the Salon, hung in the Salon des Refusés — what happens when the gatekeepers say no.

title: The Hay Wain
summary: A quiet day at the river — the unglamorous middle of the work.
